front.title: Stripe Payment Method for HTTP Payment Authentication

abstract.t: This document defines the "stripe" payment method for use with the Payment HTTP Authentication Scheme [I-D.ietf-httpauth-payment]. It specifies how clients and servers exchange payments using Stripe's payment infrastructure, supporting one-time charges, payment authorizations, and recurring subscriptions through Stripe Payment Tokens (SPTs).

section.name: Introduction
section.t: Stripe is a technology company that builds economic infrastructure for the internet. This specification defines how Stripe's payment infrastructure integrates with the Payment HTTP Authentication Scheme [I-D.ietf-httpauth-payment].

Stripe provides payment processing through Stripe Payment Tokens (SPTs), which are single-use tokens that represent payment authorization. SPTs abstract away the complexity of payment method details (cards, bank accounts, wallets) and provide a unified interface for payment acceptance.
This specification supports three payment intents:

ul.li: charge: One-time payment via Stripe Payment Token
authorize: Payment authorization for future capture

section.name: Stripe Payment Flow
section.t: The following diagram illustrates the Stripe-specific payment flow:
section.artwork: Client Server │ │ │ (1) GET /resource │ ├────────────────────────────────────────────────>│ │ │ │ (2) 402 Payment Required │ │ WWW-Authenticate: Payment method="stripe", │ │ intent="charge", request=<base64url> │ │<────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ (3) Client creates SPT via Stripe.js or API │ │ (may involve 3DS, biometrics, etc.) │ │ │ │ (4) GET /resource │ │ Authorization: Payment <credential> │ ├────────────────────────────────────────────────>│ │ │ │ (5) Server processes payment via Stripe API │ │ (PaymentIntent, Charge, or Subscription) │ │ │ │ (6) 200 OK │ │ Payment-Receipt: <receipt with charge_id> │ │<────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │

section.name: Relationship to the Payment Scheme
section.t: This document is a payment method specification as defined in Section 10.1 of [I-D.ietf-httpauth-payment]. It defines the request and payload structures for the stripe payment method, along with verification and settlement procedures.

section.name: Requirements Language
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

dd.t: A single-use token (prefixed with spt_) that represents authorization to charge a payment method. SPTs are created by clients using Stripe.js or the Stripe API and consumed by servers to process payments.
A Stripe-managed network of connected accounts that can transact with each other. Business Networks enable payments between merchants and their suppliers, partners, or service providers.
A Stripe account connected to a platform account, enabling the platform to process payments on behalf of the connected account.
A Stripe API object that tracks the lifecycle of a customer payment, from creation through settlement. Not to be confused with the HTTP Payment Auth protocol's "payment intent" parameter.
A Stripe API object that represents an intent to collect payment method details for future use, without creating an immediate charge.

section.name: Method Identifier
section.t: This specification registers the following payment method identifier:
section.sourcecode: stripe
section.t: The identifier is case-sensitive and MUST be lowercase. No sub-methods are defined by this specification.

section.name: Payment Intents
section.t: This specification defines three payment intents for use with the stripe payment method. These intents are registered in the Payment Intent Registry per Section 13.4 of [I-D.ietf-httpauth-payment].

section.name: Intent: "charge"
section.t: A one-time payment of the specified amount. The server may process the payment immediately upon receiving the SPT.
Fulfillment mechanism:

ol.li: Stripe Payment Token (SPT): The payer creates an SPT using Stripe.js

section.t: or the Stripe API, which the server uses to create a Charge or PaymentIntent via the Stripe API.

section.name: Intent: "authorize"
section.t: A payment authorization for future capture. The payer grants the server permission to charge up to the specified amount at a later time.
Required parameters:
Fulfillment mechanism:

ol.li: Stripe Payment Token with Authorization: The payer creates an SPT,

section.t: which the server uses to create a PaymentIntent with capture_method=manual. The server can capture the authorized funds within 7 days (for cards) or according to the payment method's authorization window.

section.name: Intent: "subscription"
section.t: A recurring payment authorization. The payer grants the server permission to charge a specified amount on a recurring basis.
Required parameters:

ul.li: Optional: Expiry timestamp or number of billing cycles

section.t: Fulfillment mechanism:

ol.li: Stripe Setup Intent: The payer completes a SetupIntent, providing

section.t: a payment method that can be charged on a recurring basis. The server creates a Stripe Subscription that automatically charges the payment method according to the specified schedule.

section.name: Request Schema
section.t: The request parameter in the WWW-Authenticate challenge contains a base64url-encoded JSON object. The schema is determined by the intent parameter in the challenge. Clients parse the request and construct the appropriate Stripe Payment Token or Setup Intent to fulfill it.

section.name: Charge Request
section.t: For intent="charge", the request specifies a one-time payment:

tr.td: Amount in smallest currency unit (e.g., cents)
Stripe account ID to receive funds (for Connect platforms)
Business Network ID for B2B payments
Merchant's identifier (e.g., order ID, cart ID)

section.t: Example:
section.sourcecode: { "amount": 5000, "currency": "usd", "description": "Premium API access for 1 month", "businessNetwork": "bn_1MqDcVKA5fEO2tZvKQm9g8Yj", "externalId": "order_12345" }
section.t: The client fulfills this by creating an SPT using Stripe.js:

section.name: Authorize Request
section.t: For intent="authorize", the request specifies a payment authorization:

tr.td: Maximum authorization amount in smallest currency unit
Stripe account ID to receive funds
Business Network ID for B2B payments

section.t: Example:
section.sourcecode: { "amount": 100000, "currency": "usd", "description": "AI agent compute authorization", "businessNetwork": "bn_1MqDcVKA5fEO2tZvKQm9g8Yj", "expiresAt": "2025-02-05T12:00:00Z" }
section.t: The client fulfills this by creating an SPT that the server will use with capture_method=manual:

section.name: Subscription Request
section.t: For intent="subscription", the request specifies a recurring payment:

tr.td: Amount per billing period in smallest currency unit
Stripe account ID to receive funds
Business Network ID for B2B payments
Number of billing cycles before subscription ends

section.t: Example:
section.sourcecode: { "amount": 9900, "currency": "usd", "interval": "month", "description": "Premium API subscription", "businessNetwork": "bn_1MqDcVKA5fEO2tZvKQm9g8Yj" }
section.t: The client fulfills this by creating a Setup Intent:

section.name: Credential Schema
section.t: The payload field in the Payment credential contains a base64url-encoded JSON object with Stripe-specific payment authorization data.

section.name: Charge Credential
section.t: For intent="charge", the credential contains an SPT:

tr.td: Stripe Payment Token ID (starts with spt_)

section.t: Example:

section.name: Authorize Credential
section.t: For intent="authorize", the credential contains an SPT:
Example:

section.name: Subscription Credential
section.t: For intent="subscription", the credential contains a Setup Intent ID:

tr.td: Stripe Setup Intent ID (starts with seti_)
Stripe Payment Method ID (starts with pm_)

section.t: Example:

section.name: Verification Procedure
section.t: Servers MUST verify Payment credentials according to the intent:

ol.li: Extract the spt from the credential payload
Verify the challenge ID matches the one issued
Verify the challenge has not expired
Verify the SPT has not been previously used (replay protection)
Validate the SPT exists and is valid via Stripe API (optional pre-check)

section.name: Authorize Verification
section.t: Same as charge verification (Section 8.1).

ol.li: Extract the setupIntentId and paymentMethodId from the credential
Verify the challenge ID matches
Verify the challenge has not expired
Retrieve the Setup Intent via Stripe API
Verify the Setup Intent status is succeeded
Verify the payment method is attached and valid

section.name: Challenge Binding
section.t: Servers MUST verify that the credential corresponds to the exact challenge issued. This includes validating:

section.name: Charge Settlement
section.t: Synchronous settlement:

ol.li: Server receives and verifies the credential (Section 8.1)
Server creates a Stripe PaymentIntent or Charge:
If successful, server returns 200 with Payment-Receipt header
If failed, server returns 401 with new challenge

section.t: Settlement timing:

Stripe processes payments asynchronously. Card payments typically settle within seconds, but bank transfers may take several business days. Servers SHOULD return 200 immediately after API confirmation, even if final settlement is pending.

section.name: Authorize Settlement
section.t: Deferred settlement:

ol.li: Server receives and verifies the credential (Section 8.2)
Server creates a PaymentIntent with capture_method=manual:
Server stores the PaymentIntent ID for later capture
Server returns 200 with Payment-Receipt containing authorization details
Server may capture funds later:

section.t: Authorization window:

ul.li: Other payment methods: varies by payment method

section.name: Subscription Settlement
section.t: Recurring settlement:

ol.li: Server receives and verifies the credential (Section 8.3)
Server creates a Stripe Subscription:
Server returns 200 with Payment-Receipt containing subscription ID
Stripe automatically charges the payment method on each billing cycle

section.t: Subscription lifecycle:
Servers MUST handle subscription events via webhooks:

section.name: Business Network Settlement
section.t: When businessNetwork is specified, payments flow through Stripe's Business Network infrastructure:
Business Networks enable:

section.name: Currency Support
section.t: Stripe supports 135+ currencies. Servers MUST specify currency using three-letter ISO 4217 codes (e.g., "usd", "eur", "jpy").

section.name: Payment Method Localization
section.t: SPTs abstract payment method details, but servers SHOULD consider:

ul.li: Regional payment method preferences (e.g., SEPA in Europe, ACH in US)

section.name: Description Localization
section.t: The description field SHOULD be localized to the customer's language when possible. Clients MAY include an Accept-Language header to indicate language preference.

section.name: SPT Single-Use Constraint
section.t: SPTs are single-use tokens. Servers MUST track used SPTs and reject replayed tokens. Stripe automatically prevents SPT reuse at the API level, but servers SHOULD implement their own replay protection by storing challenge IDs and verifying they haven't been previously fulfilled.

section.name: Amount Verification
section.t: Clients MUST verify the payment amount in the challenge matches their expectation before creating an SPT. The SPT itself does not encode the amount, so clients must trust the challenge parameters.
Verification checklist:

ol.li: Verify the amount matches the expected cost
Verify the currency matches the expected currency
Verify the description matches the expected service
Verify the challenge hasn't expired
Verify the server's identity (TLS certificate validation)

section.name: Business Network Authorization
section.t: When using Business Networks, clients SHOULD verify:

ul.li: The destination account (if specified) is authorized to receive payment

section.name: PCI DSS Compliance
section.t: Stripe's SPT model ensures clients never handle raw payment method details, significantly reducing PCI DSS compliance scope. Servers using this specification inherit Stripe's PCI Level 1 certification.

section.name: 3D Secure and Strong Customer Authentication
section.t: Stripe.js automatically handles 3D Secure challenges when required by the customer's bank or EU Strong Customer Authentication regulations. Clients MUST use Stripe.js or equivalent SDKs that support challenge flows.

section.name: Credential Storage
section.t: Clients MUST NOT log or persist SPTs. SPTs are bearer tokens that grant payment authorization.
Servers MUST NOT store SPTs after processing. Instead, store the resulting Stripe PaymentIntent ID, Charge ID, or Subscription ID.

section.name: HTTPS Requirement
section.t: All communication MUST use TLS 1.2 or higher. Stripe Payment Tokens MUST only be transmitted over HTTPS connections.

section.name: Payment Method Registration
section.t: This specification registers the "stripe" payment method in the Payment Method Registry per Section 13.2 of [I-D.ietf-httpauth-payment]:

section.name: Business Network Registry
section.t: This specification requests creation of a "Stripe Business Network" registry for tracking registered Business Network IDs. Registration policy: First Come First Served.

section.name: ABNF Collected
section.sourcecode:
stripe-challenge = "Payment" 1*SP "id=" quoted-string "," "realm=" quoted-string "," "method=" DQUOTE "stripe" DQUOTE "," "intent=" intent "," "request=" base64url
stripe-credential = "Payment" 1*SP base64url
intent = "charge" / "authorize" / "subscription"

section.name: Charge Example (HTTP Transport)
section.t: Step 1: Client requests resource
section.sourcecode: GET /api/generate HTTP/1.1 Host: api.example.com
section.t: Step 2: Server issues payment challenge
section.sourcecode:
HTTP/1.1 402 Payment Required WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="ch_1a2b3c4d5e", realm="api.example.com", method="stripe", intent="charge", request="eyJhbW91bnQiOjUwMDAsImN1cnJlbmN5IjoidXNkIiwiZGVzY3JpcHRpb24iOiJBSSBnZW5lcmF0aW9uIn0" Cache-Control: no-store Content-Type: application/json
{ "error": "payment_required", "message": "This resource requires payment" }
section.t: Decoded request:
Step 3: Client creates SPT and submits credential
section.sourcecode: GET /api/generate HTTP/1.1 Host: api.example.com Authorization: Payment eyJpZCI6ImNoXzFhMmIzYzRkNWUiLCJwYXlsb2FkIjoiZXlKemNIUWlPaUp6Y0hSZk1VNDBXBLJ9
section.t: Decoded credential:
Decoded payload:
Step 4: Server processes payment and returns resource
section.sourcecode:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK Payment-Receipt: eyJ0eXBlIjoiY2hhcmdlIiwiY2hhcmdlSWQiOiJjaF8xTjRadjMyZVp2S1lsbzJDUGhWUGtKbFciLCJhbW91bnQiOjUwMDAsImN1cnJlbmN5IjoidXNkIiwic3RhdHVzIjoic3VjY2VlZGVkIn0 Content-Type: text/plain
Here is your generated content...
section.t: Decoded receipt:
section.sourcecode: { "type": "charge", "chargeId": "ch_1N4Zv32eZvKYlo2CPhVPkJlW", "amount": 5000, "currency": "usd", "status": "succeeded" }

section.name: Business Network Example
section.t: Payment challenge:
section.sourcecode: HTTP/1.1 402 Payment Required WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="ch_b2b_payment", realm="supplier.example.com", method="stripe", intent="charge", request="eyJhbW91bnQiOjI1MDAwMCwiY3VycmVuY3kiOiJ1c2QiLCJidXNpbmVzc05ldHdvcmsiOiJibl8xTXFEY1ZLQTVMRU8ydFp2S1FtOWc4WWoiLCJkZXNjcmlwdGlvbiI6IlN1cHBsaWVyIHBheW1lbnQgZm9yIG9yZGVyICMxMjM0In0"
section.t: Decoded request:
section.sourcecode: { "amount": 250000, "currency": "usd", "businessNetwork": "bn_1MqDcVKA5fEO2tZvKQm9g8Yj", "description": "Supplier payment for order #1234", "destination": "acct_1MqE1vKB6gFP3uYw" }
section.t: This payment will flow through the specified Business Network, enabling automatic reconciliation and network-specific terms.

section.name: Subscription Example
section.t: Payment challenge:
section.sourcecode: HTTP/1.1 402 Payment Required WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="sub_setup_123", realm="saas.example.com", method="stripe", intent="subscription", request="eyJhbW91bnQiOjk5MDAsImN1cnJlbmN5IjoidXNkIiwiaW50ZXJ2YWwiOiJtb250aCIsImRlc2NyaXB0aW9uIjoiUHJlbWl1bSBBUEkgc3Vic2NyaXB0aW9uIn0"
section.t: Decoded request:
Client response with Setup Intent:
section.sourcecode: GET /api/subscribe HTTP/1.1 Host: saas.example.com Authorization: Payment eyJpZCI6InN1Yl9zZXR1cF8xMjMiLCJwYXlsb2FkIjoiZXlKelpYUjFjRWx1ZEdWdWRFbGtJam9pYzJWMGFWOHhUalJhZGpNeVpWcDJTMWxzYnpKRFVHaFdVR3RLYkZjaUxDSndZWGx0Wlc1MFRYV9In0
section.t: Decoded payload:
